data.title: Rio Tinto partners with AWS, becomes first customer of Nuton Technology, achieving lowest-carbon status at Johnson Camp mine with plan to produce 30,000 tonnes of refined copper in 4 years.
data.description: Rio Tinto Plc (NYSE: RIO) recently announced a two-year strategic partnership with Amazon Web Services (AWS), making AWS the initial customer of Nuton Technology. This collaboration comes after the successful large-scale implementation of Nuton's bioleaching process at the Johnson Camp copper mine in the U.S. Gunnison Copper's Johnson Camp mine in Arizona is now recognized as the most environmentally friendly primary copper producer in the U.S. The project is expected to yield approximately 30,000 tonnes of refined copper over a four-year period. As per the partnership agreement, AWS will integrate the first batch of Nuton's copper into its U.S. data centers.

data.title: Correction: Stan announced a $250k 'Dare to Dream' Challenge for self-made entrepreneurs. More details at the end.
data.description: Stan has recently initiated a 'Dare to Dream' Challenge with a budget of $250,000 to support self-made entrepreneurs. This initiative was announced on 07-Jan-2026. The challenge aims to empower individuals who have built their businesses from the ground up. Additional information regarding the challenge can be found in the full release from Stan.

data.title: Holly Picano launches on-demand AI course for small businesses, aiming to compete in an AI economy.
data.description: In Orlando, Florida, on January 15, 2026, Holly Picano, a digital marketing strategist and generative AI thought leader, introduced her new on-demand course called AI for Small Business. The course aims to aid entrepreneurs, consultants, and business owners nationally. Holly Picano, also a published author, is offering her expertise in this specialized field to provide valuable insights to help individuals advance their businesses.

data.title: Training course on Understanding and Managing Contracts (April 15-16, 2026) added to ResearchAndMarkets.com. Helps in understanding legally binding contracts with implied and express terms.
data.description: A training course titled "Understanding and Managing Contracts to Achieve Better Results Training Course" scheduled for April 15-16, 2026, in Dublin has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. This program focuses on helping participants grasp the components of a legally enforceable contract including implied terms established in statute and express terms that are negotiated and can amend statutory requirements. The interactive nature of the course aims to equip delegates with the necessary knowledge and skills for effective contract management.

data.title: Leviathan Metals to sell Timor Gold Project License to Au Gold Corp for $10,000 cash, $65,000 later, and 5,000,000 AUGC shares. LVX AUS to own 11.16% of AUGC.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia on January 15, 2026, Leviathan Metals Corp. (LVX – TSXV, LVXFF – OTC, 0GP – FSE) announced that its subsidiary, Leviathan Gold (Australia) Pty Ltd. (LVX AUS), has entered an agreement with Au Gold Corp. (AUGC, TSXV). The deal involves selling EL006278 in the Victorian Goldfields, Australia, which includes the Timor Gold Project, for $10,000 upon signing, $65,000 upon closing, and receiving 5,000,000 common shares of AUGC. This will give LVX AUS ownership of 11.16% of AUGC's outstanding shares. As of the press release date, LVX AUS did not have any previous holdings in AUGC.

data.title: Marc Hotel opens in Milwaukee - an independent 175-room hotel in downtown, connected to Baird Center. #grandopening
data.description: In Milwaukee, Marcus® Hotels & Resorts, a division of Marcus Corporation (NYSE: MCS), has opened The Marc Hotel. This independent 175-room hotel is situated in downtown Milwaukee and conveniently linked to the Baird Center through a climate-controlled skywalk. Positioned near major corporate offices, sports and entertainment venues, and lively dining options, The Marc Hotel offers a central location in the city.

data.title: PAX Services promotes Scott Voyles to Senior VP of Operations in recognition of his significant contributions.
data.description: On January 15, 2026, in Millersville, Maryland, PAX Services Group revealed the advancement of Scott Voyles to the position of Senior Vice President of Operations. This promotion comes as a result of his remarkable achievements in enhancing operational efficiency, exhibiting strong leadership qualities, and improving customer satisfaction within the company.

data.title: California home sales end strong in December with 288,200 existing, single-family homes sold at a median price of $850,680.
data.description: In December, sales of existing single-family homes reached 288,200 on a seasonally adjusted annualized basis, showing a slight increase of 0.3% from November's 287,450 and a 2.0% rise from December 2024's 282,490. The median home price across the state in December was $850,680, marking a decrease of 0.4% from $853,780 previously.

data.title: Verizon experienced a nationwide outage in the US, impacting text, voice, and data services for hours with customers upset and emergency alerts sent out. The outage's impact on Verizon stock is awaited. Verizon promises account credits but stock is under selling pressure. Customers faced inconveniences, which could lead to repercussions for the company.
data.description: A nationwide outage on Wednesday affected Verizon Communications (NYSE: VZ) in the United States, leading to disruptions in call and text services. Customers faced issues with text, voice, and data services, causing frustration and unwanted emergency alerts to be sent out. Although the outage has been resolved, the impact on Verizon's stock is anticipated, as the company plans to offer account credits to affected customers. This incident has triggered selling pressure on Verizon Communications stock, with customers expressing complaints and concerns. Such outages can have significant ramifications for users who heavily rely on their phones for various purposes, potentially leading to customer dissatisfaction or departures. Nevertheless, for some users, the outage may have only resulted in minor inconveniences. Companies often face consequences from outages, which may involve customer departures or the provision of credits to maintain customer satisfaction.

data.title: Coinbase disrupts Senate bill; Bitcoin hits $96,750, Ethereum at $3,360, XRP dips to $2.11. DCR (+30%), DASH (+10%), ICP (+10%), ZEC (+7%) surge. Ripple expands in Europe with a Luxembourg license. Pakistan and World Liberty Financial research stablecoins for remittances. Human Rights Foundation grants $1.3M in Bitcoin for freedom projects. Figure launches public equity network for on-chain stock issuance. FTX sets creditor payment timeline. Sui network back online after a six-hour stall.
data.description: Major cryptocurrencies are showing gains, with Bitcoin reaching a new 2-month high at $96,7500 and Ethereum also up 2% at $3,360. Solana remains steady at $145, while XRP dropped 1% to $2.11. Top movers include DCR (+30%), DASH (+10%), ICP (+10%), and ZEC (+7%). Monero hit an all-time high of $800 before dropping to $725. Coinbase withdrew support for the Senate's crypto bill, leading to its delay. Zcash avoided SEC action, Ripple received a Luxembourg license for expansion, and Pakistan is exploring stablecoin uses. The Human Rights Foundation awarded $1.3M in Bitcoin grants, Figure launched a new equity network, FTX is making creditor payments, and Sui resumed operation after downtime.

data.title: Citigroup's Q4 revenue at $19.87B, up 2% YoY, missing estimates. Net income down 13% to $2.5B. Adjusted net income at $3.6B.
data.description: Citigroup (NYSE: C) released its fourth-quarter results, revealing a mixed performance. The bank's fourth-quarter revenue, net of interest expense, stood at $19.87 billion, marking a 2% increase from the previous year. However, this figure fell short of analyst predictions, which had pegged it at $20.53 billion. When excluding the impact of divestiture related to the planned sale of AO Citibank in Russia, revenue saw a growth of 8%. The net income dropped by 13% year-over-year to $2.5 billion due to a $1.1 billion after-tax loss associated with exiting Russia. Adjusted net income was reported at $3.6 billion, with adjusted earnings per share also being...

data.title: Coolbrook tech company on 2026 Global Cleantech 100 list by Cleantech Group.
data.description: A technology and engineering company based in San Francisco, Coolbrook, has recently achieved recognition by being listed on the Cleantech Group’s prestigious 2026 Global Cleantech 100. This acknowledgment highlights Coolbrook’s innovative approach in the field of clean technology and engineering.

data.title: NASA's SpaceX Crew-11 successfully returns to Earth after long mission on ISS, contributing to dozens of research projects.
data.description: The latest NASA SpaceX Crew-11 mission successfully concluded as crew members aided in the progression of numerous projects supported by the ISS National Lab. These projects aimed to further life and physical sciences research, technology demonstrations, student experiments, and more. The mission safely ended with a splashdown off the coast of Kennedy Space Center in Florida on January 15, 2026. An array of essential tasks were accomplished, contributing significantly to advancements in space exploration.

data.title: Crude oil drops, US initial jobless claims down. Dow up 0.80% to 49,543.16, NASDAQ climbs 1.03% to 23,714.62, S&P 500 rises 0.74% to 6,977.92.
data.description: Midway through trading, U.S. stocks saw a boost with the Nasdaq Composite rising by approximately 1%. The Dow climbed by 0.80% to reach 49,543.16, while the NASDAQ surged by 1.03% to 23,714.62. The S&P 500 also experienced gains, increasing by 0.74% to 6,977.92. Information technology shares led the way, rising by 1.5%, while health care stocks dipped by 1.5% on Thursday. Initial jobless claims in the U.S. saw a decrease of 9,000 from the previous week, totaling 198,000 in the week ending Jan. 10, surpassing market estimates of 215,000. Callan JMB Inc. (NASDAQ: CJMB) witnessed a significant rise of 208%, reaching $3.45, following the announcement of a strategic teaming agreement with Biostax Corp, now known as Attune Biotech, to offer federal oversight services. For more details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Xulon Press author shares divine revelations. "A Sudden Switch" delves into Christian life insights.
data.description: Xulon Press introduces a collection of teachings exploring aspects of Christian living. In ORLANDO, Fla., on Jan. 15, 2026, Author Oberziner VonChele discloses spiritual revelations received as a devout follower of Jesus Christ in the book "A Sudden Switch: Walking In Steadiness In A Season Of Preparation."

data.title: PerfectQuote partners with Guardian to improve digital distribution for group benefits, enhancing broker connectivity on Jan 15, 2026.
data.description: In Austin, Texas on January 15, 2026, PerfectQuote, Inc., a prominent digital quoting and distribution platform for group health and ancillary benefits, revealed a significant partnership with The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America® ("Guardian"). This collaboration signifies a new milestone for both companies as they join forces to enhance their services.
